step_by_step_thinking:
Clinical Reasoning:
Dedicated discharge coordinators are part of an interprofessional team that enhances communication, coordinates follow-up care, educates patients, and addresses social determinants of health—all proven to lower 30-day and 90-day readmission rates. Specific examples include the Care Transitions Intervention (CTI), which demonstrated a statistically significant reduction in both short- and long-term readmissions via a trained discharge nurse coach who visited patients pre-discharge, at home, and via phone calls. Similarly, a randomized controlled trial found that a multidisciplinary team—including a nurse for discharge planning—resulted in only 31% post-discharge utilization vs. 44% in controls, indicating superior outcomes with structured coordination. Since preventing avoidable readmissions improves patient quality of life, reduces costs, and supports continuity of care, a dedicated coordinator directly contributes to enhanced discharge quality.
Answer Choice: \boxed{A} Yes, a dedicated discharge coordinator improves the quality of hospital discharge because empirical evidence shows reductions in readmission rates and improvements in patient outcomes through structured coordination, patient education, and follow-up engagement.
\boxed{A} Based on the evidence, a dedicated discharge coordinator improves the quality of hospital discharge by facilitating effective communication, supporting patient education, coordinating follow-up care, and reducing readmission rates — all of which directly contribute to safer and more effective transitions of care. Therefore, the best-supported answer is \boxed{A}.
\boxed{A}
**Final Answer:** \boxed{A}
